cases.title: Academic Admin generates Scheduled sessions from class section schedule template
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with institution scope.
Active term, course, room, and lecturer exist for section creation.
No existing section with code SE101-01 in the target term.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sections with sectionCode, termId, courseId, lecturerUserId, defaultRoomId, and scheduleTemplate (dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes) per API design §4.2.
Inspect the success response for generated Scheduled session count when session generation is requested.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={sectionId} as AcademicAdmin.
Assert each returned session has state Scheduled, scheduledStartAt, scheduledEndAt derived from duration, and roomId matching section default or template.
cases.expected: WF-01 step 3 completes: sessions are persisted in Scheduled state with scheduled start time, duration, and room bound to the section; lecturer can later list upcoming sessions (FR-10 prerequisite).

cases.title: Academic Admin manually creates a one-off Scheduled session when timetable changes
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
A class section exists with known classSectionId and default room.
A makeup session date/time is outside the recurring template pattern.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions with classSectionId, scheduledStartAt, durationMinutes, and optional roomId override for the changed venue.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard {data, meta, error} envelope.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={classSectionId}&date={sessionDate} and locate the manually created session.
Verify the session state is Scheduled and roomId reflects the override when provided.
cases.expected: Manual session is created in Scheduled state with correct scheduled times, duration, and room; it appears in section session listings alongside template-generated sessions.

cases.title: M03 Session Lifecycle persists ClassSession row with section FK and Scheduled initial state
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions and class_sections tables.
A valid classSectionId exists with FK-enforced section row.
AcademicAdmin auth context is available to M03.
steps: Create a session through M03 create-session command or POST /v1/class-sessions API path.
Query class_sessions table and assert class_section_id FK, scheduled_start_at, scheduled_end_at, room_id, and state = Scheduled.
Invoke getSessionState(sessionId) and assert state Scheduled with no openedAt or closedAt timestamps.
Attempt create with non-existent classSectionId and observe rejection without orphan session row.
cases.expected: ClassSession row is committed with required attributes and section FK; initial state is Scheduled with no attendance-window timestamps; invalid section reference is rejected.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions returns 200 envelope with Scheduled session payload
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
Request body includes classSectionId, scheduledStartAt, and durationMinutes per domain model §4.3.
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions with Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error}.
Assert data contains classSessionId (or id), classSectionId, state Scheduled, scheduledStartAt, scheduledEndAt, and roomId when supplied.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data reflects a newly created Scheduled session with persisted schedule and room attributes.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions list returns paginated sessions with state filter
preconditions: At least one Scheduled session exists for a class section in lecturer scope.
Lecturer assigned to the section is authenticated.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={sectionId}&state=Scheduled with Lecturer Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard envelope with data array.
Assert each row includes classSessionId, state, scheduledStartAt, and section reference.
Assert meta.pagination includes page, pageSize, totalItems, and totalPages per API conventions §2.5.
cases.expected: List response is HTTP 200 with paginated session collection filtered by classSectionId and state; Lecturer view scope is satisfied for FR-06 view permission.

cases.title: Cancel command transitions Scheduled session to Cancelled
preconditions: A class session exists in Scheduled state with known sessionId.
AcademicAdmin (or authorized Lecturer per policy) auth context is available.
Session has never been opened (no openedAt).
steps: Record beforeState state Scheduled for the target session.
Invoke POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/cancel with Authorization and Idempotency-Key.
Query persistence and record afterState.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={sectionId}&state=Cancelled and verify the session appears with Cancelled badge semantics.
cases.expected: beforeState Scheduled transitions to afterState Cancelled; no QR tokens are issued and open attendance is blocked per SM-01 cancel invariants (SMI-INV-05).

cases.title: Session list pagination honors page and pageSize boundaries for lecturer dashboard
preconditions: Database seed contains more than 25 Scheduled sessions across assigned sections for one Lecturer.
Lecturer auth context is established.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions?page=1&pageSize=25 as assigned Lecturer and record totalItems and row count.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions?page=2&pageSize=25 and assert a disjoint result set from page 1.
Request a page beyond totalPages and assert empty data array with consistent totalItems.
Verify all returned sessions belong to sections assigned to the Lecturer (no cross-scope leakage).
cases.expected: Pagination returns correct page boundaries, stable totalItems across pages, empty data when page exceeds totalPages, and scope-filtered rows only.

cases.title: Non-admin roles are denied session create and cancel mutations
preconditions: Student, Lecturer (assigned to a section), and ITAdmin test accounts exist with valid tokens.
A Scheduled session exists in a section the Lecturer is assigned to.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions as Student, Lecturer, and ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/cancel as Student and ITAdmin.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each denied request.
Query class_sessions table to confirm no new or cancelled rows were created by unauthorized actors.
cases.expected: Unauthorized roles receive 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope without creating or cancelling sessions; Lecturer retains read access via GET /v1/class-sessions but cannot mutate session lifecycle (FR-06 actor boundary).

cases.title: Session create rejects scheduled end before start with validation error
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
A valid classSectionId exists.
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions with scheduledStartAt 2026-07-02T10:00:00Z and durationMinutes yielding scheduledEndAt before start (e.g. negative duration or explicit invalid end).
Read HTTP status and error.code from the response envelope.
Query class_sessions table to confirm no row was created for the invalid payload.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 400 with InvalidPayload or equivalent validation error; no ClassSession row is persisted for incoherent schedule times.

cases.title: Open and cancel rejected on terminal or invalid session states
preconditions: A Cancelled session and a Closed session exist with known sessionIds.
Assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin tokens are available.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{cancelledSessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{openSessionId}/cancel for a session already in Open state.
Read HTTP status and error.code (expect 409 InvalidSessionTransition) for each.
Verify session states remain unchanged in persistence.
cases.expected: Cancelled → Open and Open → Cancelled attempts return HTTP 409 InvalidSessionTransition per SM-01 invalid transitions; terminal Cancelled and Closed semantics are preserved.

cases.title: AcademicAdmin authenticates then creates session end to end
preconditions: AcademicAdmin credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
A valid classSectionId exists.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with AcademicAdmin credentials and capture accessToken and roles from the 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with the bearer token and assert AcademicAdmin identity and scope.
Send POST /v1/class-sessions with the bearer token and valid schedule payload.
Assert the create returns HTTP 200 with data.state Scheduled within the authenticated admin scope.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken; GET /v1/me returns AcademicAdmin identity; subsequent session create succeeds in Scheduled state within authorized scope.

cases.title: Lecturer PG-04 session list shows status badges and enrolled count for generated sessions
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Assigned section has at least one Scheduled session generated from timetable or manual create.
Enrollments exist for the section.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions (PG-04) and verify DataTable renders with session columns (section code, start time, state badge, enrolled count).
Apply state filter chip Scheduled and confirm only Scheduled sessions display.
Locate a Scheduled session row and verify StatusBadge shows Scheduled with open/view-roster quick actions gated appropriately.
If a Cancelled session exists in fixture, verify Cancelled badge renders and open CTA is disabled or hidden per UI states §2.
cases.expected: PG-04 displays paginated session list with Scheduled/Open/Closed/Cancelled badges, enrolled count, and state-appropriate actions; Lecturer view-only access to FR-06-generated sessions is satisfied.

cases.title: Academic Admin FRM-04 section create with schedule template generates sessions
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Admin navigation exposes class sections at /admin/class-sections.
Active term, course, lecturer, and room fixtures are available.
steps: Navigate to /admin/class-sections and open Create section (FRM-04).
Complete section code, term, course, lecturer, default room, and schedule template fields (dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes).
Submit the form and observe success feedback including generated Scheduled session count when applicable.
Open the section detail or sessions sub-view and confirm Scheduled sessions appear with correct start times without full page reload where SPA routing applies.
cases.expected: FRM-04 create flow with schedule template persists the section and generates Scheduled sessions visible in admin UI; session rows reflect scheduled start, duration, and room per FR-06.
